test.name: Danish patronymic parentage plan includes the conscription (lægdsruller) item
test.description: blyeberg-first-plan: q_001 (who was the father of Mikkel Nielsen Blyeberg, b. 1779 Stagstrup, Thisted, Denmark?) has no plan yet, but a `localities` entry (loc_001, Denmark / Thisted) is already present — the orchestrator ran locality-guide first, and that entry records the lægdsruller fact (males enrolled under the father's name from 1789 = direct parentage evidence). research-plan is a pure consumer: it reads that entry and must produce a sound first plan that includes a dedicated conscription-roll item alongside the baptism/church and parents'-marriage items. Guards the record-type-guide 'sons in conscription countries' improvement (issue #574: the lægdsruller were never planned).

input.user_message: Create the first research plan for q_001 — who was the father of Mikkel Nielsen Blyeberg?

judge_context: A `localities` entry (loc_001) for Denmark / Thisted is ALREADY in research.json — the orchestrator ran locality-guide before research-plan, and its quirks record the lægdsruller fact (males enrolled under the father's name from 1789 = direct parentage evidence) plus the patronymic and browse-only-register notes. The skill should READ that entry; it should NOT call wiki_search / wiki_place_page / place_population (research-plan no longer holds those tools). Do NOT fail it for not surveying.

The plan MUST include at least one plan item targeting the Danish military levy rolls / conscription registers (lægdsruller) — record_type 'military', or an item whose jurisdiction/rationale unambiguously names the levy rolls. The localities entry names 'Denmark, Military Levying Rolls (Lægdsruller), 1789-1861', and research-plan may confirm it via collections_search / volume_search.

The levy-roll item's rationale should gesture at why the rolls bear on parentage — males are enrolled under the father's name (the father's entry directly above the son's), making the roll direct evidence of the father for a male subject born 1779 (enrolled from the system's 1789 start). An adequate gesture passes; verbatim enumeration is not required.
If NO plan item targets the conscription/levy rolls, score Record type selection as 1 (fail): omitting the lægdsruller for a male Danish subject is the specific gap this test guards.

The plan should also cover the standard parentage record types for the jurisdiction — the Stagstrup parish register/baptism (the localities entry notes it is only ~3% indexed, so noting a browse fallback is good practice) and, per the standing parentage guidance, the parents' marriage. These complement the levy-roll item rather than replace it; grade their presence generously.
